# Task Log - Fantasy Theme for Fancy UI
**Date:** December 20, 2025, 4:36 PM

## Task Description
Implement fantasy-themed UI for the Fancy UI when the game type is "Fantasy".

## Requirements
- If a TextGame is the type "Fantasy" and uses the Fancy UI, make the user interface fantasy themed
- The theme should be applied automatically based on cartridge metadata

## Implementation Details

### Files Modified

#### 1. `GameInterface\TextGameUI\FancyUI\TextGameWindowFancy.xaml.cs`
- Added `_cartridgeMetadata` field to store cartridge information
- Added new constructor overload that accepts `CartridgeData metadata` parameter
- Created `ApplyTheme()` method to check game type and apply appropriate theme
- Created `ApplyFantasyTheme()` method that applies fantasy-themed colors:
  - **Window Background**: Deep mystical purple/black (RGB: 20, 15, 35)
  - **Main Borders**: Magical purple (RGB: 147, 112, 219)
  - **Panel Backgrounds**: Dark enchanted purple (RGB: 35, 25, 55)
  - **Output Area**: Very dark mystical background (RGB: 25, 15, 40)
  - **Text Colors**: Soft lavender for main text (RGB: 220, 200, 255)
  - **Headers/Labels**: Golden mystical color (RGB: 255, 215, 100)
  - **Status Cards**: Enchanted purple (RGB: 45, 35, 70) with orchid borders (RGB: 186, 85, 211)
  - **Send Button**: Purple gradient (Medium Purple to Medium Orchid)
  - **Title**: Updates to show cartridge title
- Created `UpdateInventoryTheme()` method to apply fantasy colors to inventory items
- Modified `UpdateInventory()` method to apply theme-appropriate colors to inventory cards dynamically

#### 2. `GameInterface\TextGameUI\FancyUI\TextGameWindowFancy.xaml`
- Added `x:Name` attributes to all major UI elements so they can be referenced in code-behind:
  - `StatusBorder`, `OutputBorder`, `InventoryBorder`, `HistoryBorder`
  - `LocationCard`, `ExitsCard`, `StatsCard`
  - `LocationLabel`, `ExitsLabel`, `StatsLabel`
  - `LocationText`, `ExitsText`, `MovesText`, `ScoreText`, `ItemCountText`
  - `OutputScrollViewer`, `GameOutputText`
  - `CommandPrompt`, `CommandInput`, `SendButton`
  - `InventoryPanel`, `InventoryCountText`, `CommandHistoryText`

#### 3. `GameInterface\CartridgeSelection\CartridgeListWindow.xaml.cs`
- Modified `CartridgeItem_MouseDown()` method to pass metadata to `TextGameWindowFancy`
- Changed from `new TextGameWindowFancy(startingRoom)` to `new TextGameWindowFancy(startingRoom, metadata)`

## Theme Color Palette

### Fantasy Theme Colors
- **Primary Background**: RGB(20, 15, 35) - Deep mystical darkness
- **Panel Background**: RGB(35, 25, 55) - Dark enchanted purple
- **Card Background**: RGB(45, 35, 70) - Lighter mystical purple
- **Output Background**: RGB(25, 15, 40) - Very dark magical atmosphere
- **Primary Border**: RGB(147, 112, 219) - Medium purple
- **Secondary Border**: RGB(186, 85, 211) - Medium orchid
- **Primary Text**: RGB(220, 200, 255) - Soft lavender
- **Header/Label Text**: RGB(255, 215, 100) - Golden mystical
- **Empty Inventory**: RGB(120, 100, 150) - Muted purple

### Default (Non-Fantasy) Theme Colors
- Maintains original VS Code-inspired dark theme
- Uses blues, grays, and standard colors

## Testing
- Build completed successfully
- No compilation errors
- Fantasy theme will apply when:
  - UIType = "Fancy"
  - Type = "Fantasy"
- Example: The "Enchanted Forest" cartridge should now display with fantasy theme

## Future Enhancements
- Could add additional themes for other game types (e.g., Science Fiction, Horror)
- Could add custom fonts for fantasy theme
- Could add subtle animations or particle effects
- Could add theme-specific icons or decorative elements
